verdicts.note: The diff skips terminal allow evaluation whenever shell chaining/substitution/redirection markers are present, while preserving deny-first evaluation. The added tests cover the reported allow-prefix chaining bypass cases.

HTTPError is now caught before URLError and fails closed in both interactive and unattended modes. The interactive ask fallback is limited to liveness-style URLError/OSError/TimeoutError cases when a token is configured.
Non-object hook payloads and non-object judge responses now return deny/exit 2 instead of raising. main() also has a final exception guard that emits a fail-closed deny.

The diff fixes the host-boundary prefix bypass for curl/wget and adds WebFetch plus several git/scp-style deny patterns. It still does not fully cover the finding's common network surfaces, such as direct ssh commands, scp-style git remotes like git@evil.example:repo, and Python -c network forms; the requested userinfo tests are also absent.

Path escape checks now resolve relative paths against the supplied repo_root and use realpath to follow existing symlinks. The added tests cover both repo-root-relative resolution and symlink escapes.

The diff commits .claude/settings.json with a PreToolUse hook pointing at gauntlet-judge-hook and adds a static wiring test. The no-token ask behavior matches the ratified safe-by-default disposition for plain sessions.

The adapter now uses a 5 second per-call timeout, max_tokens, and temperature 0, which is an improvement. However ApiAdapter still defaults to two schema retries, so the classifier path can take up to three 5 second attempts and exceed the 8 second hook timeout.

The integration tests now look for denial evidence instead of only checking file absence. The helper still accepts any nonzero command_execution exit code, and result.text markers alone can satisfy it, so it does not strictly prove both that the write command was issued and that the sandbox returned an operation-denied error.
Accepted the ratified reject disposition: default fail-closed without a configured LLM is the intended P2 posture, with the bounded opt-in classifier path left for configured runs.

summary: Most fixes address the reviewed issues. Remaining partials are limited to incomplete outbound-network surface coverage and two test/timeout proof gaps in the P2.1 diff.
